skill.name: explore-code
skill.description: Rigor Improve implementation leaf skill for auditable candidate implementation in deep learning research repositories. Use when the researcher explicitly authorizes exploratory work on an isolated branch or worktree to transplant modules, adapt a backbone, add LoRA or adapter layers, replace a head, or stitch together meaningful low-risk migration ideas with rollback-aware records in `explore_outputs/`. Do not use for end-to-end exploration orchestration on top of `current_research`, trusted baseline reproduction, conservative debugging, environment setup, verified contribution claims, or default repository analysis.
skill.summary: Exploratory ML code change planning skill for deep learning research repositories.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 38 patterns including 'Ruby/shell backtick execution', 'path traversal', and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but all are false positives. The backticks are markdown code spans in documentation, not shell execution. Path references are relative markdown paths. No actual subprocess calls, eval, network requests, or cryptographic operations exist. The skill uses only standard Python libraries (argparse, json, re, pathlib) for legitimate ML research code change planning.

content.user_title: Plan Safe ML Code Changes with Rollback Awareness
content.value_statement: Deep learning researchers need to explore code modifications without breaking trusted baselines. This skill creates conservative, auditable change plans with explicit rollback paths for isolated worktrees.
actual_capabilities: Generates conservative exploratory code-change plans from repository analysis
Identifies candidate edit targets using ML-aware scoring (model, backbone, adapter, LoRA patterns)
Creates rollback-aware change documentation for isolated branches
Produces structured output bundles including CHANGESET.md, SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, and status.json
Supports module transplant, backbone adaptation, and adapter layer insertion workflows
Maintains comparison anchors to current_research baseline for attribution
limitations: Only plans code changes; does not execute or apply modifications to the repository
Requires explicit researcher authorization for exploratory work
Not suitable for trusted baseline reproduction or conservative debugging tasks
Does not perform end-to-end experiment orchestration

use_cases.title: LoRA Adapter Integration Planning
use_cases.description: Plan the addition of LoRA adapter layers to an existing model architecture while maintaining rollback options.

use_cases.title: Backbone Module Transplant
use_cases.description: Create a change plan for transplanting modules from one backbone architecture to another with proper attribution.
use_cases.target_user: Computer vision researchers adapting pre-trained models

use_cases.title: Head Replacement Strategy
use_cases.description: Plan the replacement of classification or detection heads while preserving evaluation metric semantics.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Exploration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan exploratory code changes for adding LoRA adapters to the ViT backbone in this repository. Focus on isolated branch work with clear rollback paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for adapter insertion planning

prompt_templates.title: Module Transplant
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a change plan to transplant the segmentation head from model A to model B, maintaining source attribution and comparison anchors to current_research.

prompt_templates.title: Head Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan replacing the classification head with a detection head while keeping evaluation metrics unchanged and documenting the rollback procedure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Task family transition with metric preservation

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Change Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a combined change: LoRA insertion plus backbone adaptation plus head replacement, with prioritized code tracks and smoke validation steps.

output_examples.input: Plan LoRA adapter insertion for ViT backbone in ./models/vit/
output: Candidate edit targets: models/vit/encoder.py, models/vit/config.yaml, train/configs/lora.yaml
- Stay anchored to the classification task family while planning exploratory edits
- Preserve vit-baseline-2024 as the comparison anchor for all code changes
- Inspect candidate model files first: models/vit/encoder.py, models/vit/config.yaml
- Keep python train.py aligned with any exploratory code path changes
- Prefer one reversible module-level adaptation before broader rewrites

output_examples.input: Create transplant plan from segmentation repo to detection repo
output: Output bundle: explore_outputs/CHANGESET.md, explore_outputs/SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, explore_outputs/COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md, explore_outputs/TOP_RUNS.md, explore_outputs/status.json
Change type: module-transplant-shim with rollback: Delete the shim and return the call-site to the baseline symbol
Protected zone: evaluation and metric files remain unchanged

best_practices: Always work on an isolated branch or worktree when using this skill
Record current_research and experiment branch identifiers before making changes
Keep exploratory work separate from the trusted baseline at all times
Prefer the smallest viable adaptation over broad rewrites to minimize risk
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for trusted baseline reproduction or conservative debugging
Avoid freeform large-scale refactors that lack rollback documentation
Do not claim reproduction success from exploratory candidate changes

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It creates conservative, auditable plans for exploratory ML code changes including module transplants, LoRA insertions, and head replacements with rollback documentation.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only plans changes and produces documentation. It does not execute or apply modifications to the repository.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have explicit authorization to explore code changes on an isolated branch, especially for adapter insertion, backbone adaptation, or module transplantation.

faq.question: What output files does it produce?
faq.answer: It generates explore_outputs/CHANGESET.md, SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md, TOP_RUNS.md, and status.json.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with my baseline?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill enforces isolation from trusted baselines and requires explicit authorization. Results are treated as candidates, not trusted conclusions.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code environments.
